title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in San Antonio, Texas

linkedin:
If your client was involved in a semi-truck crash in San Antonio, Texas, one critical piece of evidence may already be at risk: the truck's black box, also known as the ECM or EDR (Event Data Recorder).

Commercial trucks are equipped with onboard data recorders that capture speed, braking behavior, engine performance, hours of service, and more—often in the seconds before impact. This data can be the difference between a strong case and an uphill battle. But here's the urgent reality: trucking companies are not always required to preserve this data indefinitely, and it can be overwritten or lost within days after a crash.

For attorneys in our network of 800+ law firms across Texas and beyond, acting fast on spoliation letters and preservation demands is essential in these cases. WreckMatch connects injured victims with experienced truck accident attorneys who know exactly how to move quickly on evidence preservation.

If you're an attorney looking to receive qualified truck accident referrals in San Antonio or across Texas, or if you're a victim who needs help now, visit us today.

reddit_title: Semi-truck crash in San Antonio – does the truck's black box data actually matter and how do you preserve it?
reddit_body:
After a commercial semi-truck crash, most people don't realize the truck itself may have been recording data the entire time. The ECM (Engine Control Module) and EDR (Event Data Recorder) capture things like vehicle speed, brake application, throttle position, and hours of service logs. In a disputed liability case, this data can be extremely valuable evidence.

The biggest issue is timing. Unlike passenger car EDRs, commercial truck data isn't always automatically preserved after a crash. Trucking companies often have rapid response teams on scene quickly, and data can be overwritten during normal truck operation if the vehicle isn't immediately taken out of service. An attorney can send a spoliation letter demanding preservation, but this needs to happen fast—sometimes within 24 to 72 hours.

If you're dealing with this situation, the most practical advice is to consult a truck accident attorney as soon as possible, ideally one with specific experience in commercial vehicle litigation in Texas. Some people find it hard to know where to start, and services like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys who handle exactly these kinds of cases in San Antonio and across Texas.
